title: How Long Does It Take to Design a Website?
excerpt: One of the most common questions before any web project kicks off is: "When will it be done?" Timelines vary enormously based on project size, process, and how quickly you provide feedback. This guide gives you realistic expectations so you can plan accordingly.
content:
There's no single answer to how long a website takes to design — but there are useful benchmarks that can help you plan your project timeline. Here's what to expect at each level.

Simple Brochure Website (3–5 Pages)

Timeline: 2–4 weeks

A simple website covering your home, about, services, and contact pages can be designed relatively quickly if you have your content and branding ready. Expect 2 weeks for design and another 1–2 weeks for development and testing.

Small Business Website (5–10 Pages)

Timeline: 4–8 weeks

More pages mean more design decisions, more content to lay out, and more review rounds. A quality small business website typically takes 1–2 months from kickoff to launch.

E-Commerce Website

Timeline: 8–16 weeks

E-commerce adds significant complexity — product catalog design, cart and checkout flows, payment integration, and user account management. Expect 2–4 months for a properly built online store.

Custom Web Application

Timeline: 3–6+ months

Complex applications with user authentication, data management, APIs, and custom functionality require extensive planning, design, and development. These projects are rarely rushed without compromising quality.

What Causes Delays?

The most common causes of timeline overrun:

  • Slow client feedback and approvals

  • Content (copy, images) not ready on time

  • Scope creep — adding features mid-project

  • Unexpected technical challenges

  • Poor project management


How to Keep Your Project on Track

  • Provide a clear brief at the start
  • Have your content ready before design begins
  • Give timely, specific feedback at each stage
  • Respect the agreed scope

We Deliver on Time

At Bluewaves Technology, we set clear, realistic timelines and keep you informed at every stage. Our project management process is designed to eliminate surprises and keep your project moving.
